cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. The user is asking about the three different jars of low fat milk mentioned in the passage. The options are about their fat content and prices. First, I'll recall the relevant part of the text. The passage states that the store has three jars of low fat milk. Each jar has different labeling: one says "1 percent fat," another "99 percent fat free," and the third has "LOW FAT" in big letters with "1%" in small letters. The key point here is that the passage explicitly mentions, "As you can see, all the milk has the same amount of fat. The milk is all the same. However, in this store the three jars of milk cost three different amounts of money." So the main facts here are: 1. All three jars have the same amount of fat. 2. The jars are priced differently despite having the same fat content. Now, looking at the options: A) one has more fat than the other two – This contradicts the passage which says all have the same amount. So this is incorrect. B) they all cost the same amount of money – The passage clearly states they cost different amounts. So B is incorrect. C) one has less fat than the others – Again, all have the same fat content.
